title: Checking if an Element Exists in an ArrayList

content.problemStatement:
In this lesson, we'll learn how to check whether a specific element exists in an ArrayList using the `contains(value)` method.

---

The `contains()` method is very simple:
- It takes one parameter: the object you want to check for
- It returns `true` if the element exists in the ArrayList
- It returns `false` if the element doesn't exist

For example, if we have an ArrayList of fruits:
```java
ArrayList fruits = new ArrayList<>();
fruits.add("Apple");
fruits.add("Banana");
fruits.add("Orange");
```

We can check if "Banana" exists:
```java
boolean hasBanana = fruits.contains("Banana");  // returns true
boolean hasGrape = fruits.contains("Grape");    // returns false
boolean hasBanana = fruits.contains("banana");  // returns false because the check is case-sensitive for Strings
```
